nodes.headline: Make offer owners cannot refuse by aligning economics with growth.
nodes.text:
# Acquisition Structure

> Make offer owners cannot refuse by aligning economics with growth.

## Framework
- Solo multiple: 4x
- Consolidated multiple: 10-12x
- Offer: 7x as incentive to consolidate

## Claims
- Solo dental practice sale multiple is 4x EBITDA, consolidated multiple is 10-12x — [make_an_offer_so_good_they_feel_stupid_saying_no_lpA7Uhav8qA.txt]

## Connections
- Aligning economics in acquisition offers incentivizes growth; owner equity increases unlock expansion discipline. _(connects to: Ownership Equity Incentive Structure)_

---
_Source: make_an_offer_so_good_they_feel_stupid_saying_no_lpA7Uhav8qA.txt · 1 claim(s)_

nodes.text: Businesses don't need to become AI companies but must use AI as a tool across all functions. AI makes operations better, faster, cheaper, and less risky when properly implemented with business acumen overlaid on technical capability.

nodes.headline: Founder has built a 2–3 hour/week business by hiring right; now comfort is stopping him, not capability
nodes.text:
# Comfort as Constraint

> Founder has built a 2–3 hour/week business by hiring right; now comfort is stopping him, not capability

## Key Numbers
- Current revenue: $6M at 2–3 hours/week
- Target: $100M
- Constraint: willingness to go all-in again

## Key Takeaway
- Already proved he can scale (did it first 5 years); now comfortable and doesn't want to disturb it

## Claims
- Business currently runs on 2–3 hours/week because founder replaced himself everywhere; comfort is real constraint to $100M — [i_want_to_grow_my_business_but_i_don_t_want_to_sacrifice_fam_iq4VNdOAfWM.txt]

## Connections
- Both identify that founder mindset, not financial resources, limits growth—hesitation to hire and comfort-seeking are psychological constraints masquerading as external barriers. _(connects to: Leadership And Founder Capacity)_

---
_Source: i_want_to_grow_my_business_but_i_don_t_want_to_sacrifice_fam_iq4VNdOAfWM.txt · 1 claim(s)_

nodes.node_id: edge:Acquisition Vs Organic Growth|Organic Growth Model
nodes.text: Organic growth requires better founders; acquisitions need deal flow but organic scaling shows founder quality.

nodes.headline: Scaling from 1 to 5+ locations requires owner business time; can't be done if owner is 80% clinical
nodes.text:
# Multiple Location Expansion

> Scaling from 1 to 5+ locations requires owner business time; can't be done if owner is 80% clinical

## Key Takeaway
- Goal: $20M across multiple locations
- Requirement: owner as business operator, not clinician
- Step 1: hire physician, free time; Step 2: fix sales/ops

## Claims
- Goal of $20M across multiple locations requires business owner time, not physician time; the limiting factor is attention, not capital or revenue ceiling — [3m_med_spa_owner_wants_to_scale_to_20m_rqJM6mFhyes.txt]

## Connections
- Multi-location scaling requires owner time; split focus kills margins and growth. _(connects to: Location Expansion Dynamics)_
- Franchising uses capital to scale quickly, but requires founder first to escape operational roles before expansion. _(connects to: Franchising Strategy)_
- Add locations only after model self-propagates; scaling 1 to 5+ requires owner business time and can't execute if founder is 80% clinical. _(connects to: Location Scaling)_
- Geographic expansion requires owner business time; can't scale locations if owner is delivering 80% of work. _(connects to: Market Expansion And Geographic Scaling)_

---
_Source: 3m_med_spa_owner_wants_to_scale_to_20m_rqJM6mFhyes.txt · 1 claim(s)_

nodes.headline: Discretionary effort requires supportive home life; leaders enable rest and create conditions for team to achieve potential.
nodes.text:
# Team & Personal Life

> Discretionary effort requires supportive home life; leaders enable rest and create conditions for team to achieve potential.

## Framework
- Unsupportive spouse/partner = diminished professional potential (widespread, not rare)
- Good home front = full brain access for 'the hunt'
- Unsupportive environment → cutting corners, avoiding bigger projects, smaller goals

## Key Takeaway
- Hard workers will run themselves into the ground; leader must visibly grant permission to rest
- Gauge when people are close to cracking; proactively tell them to take day/weekend/unplug
- Making history is rare; unlock discretionary effort by supporting team members' home environments

## Examples
- Told team during book launch: you will be able to say you made history when looking back

## Claims
- If spouse or significant other is unsupportive of work, you will not achieve your potential; good home front allows full brain access for the hunt — [26_harsh_lessons_i_learned_in_2025_YONIUpk-kAs.txt]
- Hard workers run themselves into the ground; leaders must visibly tell them to rest and unplug so they feel permission to stop — [26_harsh_lessons_i_learned_in_2025_YONIUpk-kAs.txt]
- Making history is rare; unlock discretionary effort by ensuring team members have supportive home environments and spouses — [26_harsh_lessons_i_learned_in_2025_YONIUpk-kAs.txt]

---
_Source: 26_harsh_lessons_i_learned_in_2025_YONIUpk-kAs.txt · 3 claim(s)_
